1.title: The Forest Feels Wrong
1.text:
The forest is too quiet.
Not even birds.
Something is off.

choices.result: You push deeper into the forest. The silence only gets heavier, like the trees are watching.
You voice concern and suggest turning back. The group hesitates, but keeps moving.
You scan the surroundings carefully. Nothing visible moves, but something still feels wrong.

2.title: Strange Tracks
2.text:
The hunt continues.
Silence sticks.
Even the hunters feel it.

Then, muddy stream.
Massive footprints.
Too big for anything normal.

Something passed here. Recently.

choices.result: You kneel by the tracks. The size isn't natural. Whatever passed here didn't care about leaving evidence.
You warn the group. The tension rises instantly, but everyone becomes more alert.
You move ahead alone. The forest feels closer when you're isolated.

3.title: First Contact
3.text:
Movement in the trees.

A massive lion-like creature steps out.
It locks onto you.

No warning.
No hesitation.
Just danger.

choices.result: You pull back with the group. For once, survival matters more than pride.
You step forward without hesitation. The creature reacts instantly.
You draw its attention. The group gets a window to move, even if it costs you.

secret_peaceful_event.title: Aftermath
secret_peaceful_event.text:
It works.
The group survives.
No chase.
No more threats.

The forest lets you go.

PEACEFUL_END.title: A Life That Stayed
PEACEFUL_END.text:
You chose to fall back.

Not out of fear. Out of understanding.

The hunt ended. The forest faded into routine.

The village moved on like nothing had changed.

And slowly, neither did you.

Work. Familiar faces. Quiet mornings.

The forest became a story people stopped telling.

Years passed without asking anything from you.

No glory. No ruin.
Just days that kept repeating.

One afternoon, you sat in your backyard.

Warm air. Still world.

And you stayed there.

No struggle.
No final thought.

Just silence that did not need to end.

3_5.title: Split Path
3_5.text:
Two paths.

Left: silent.
Right: disturbed.

Choose.

head_event.title: The Stranger
head_event.text:
The path stretches forward.

The forest is thinning now. Not fading. Ending.

Then... someone appears.

A man stands ahead on the road.

No armor. No weapon raised.
Clean clothes. Calm posture.
Like he belongs here.

He looks at you.

Noticing everything.
Your wounds.
Your weapon.
Your condition.

"…Interesting."

You tighten your grip instantly.

"Don't."

Calm voice. No threat.

"You're injured. Exhausted.
And days away from the nearest wilderness checkpoint."

He studies you.

"You don't know what that is."

Silence.

"You came through that?"

You nod.

"That shouldn't be possible."

He steps closer.

"What settlement are you from?"

You answer.

His expression changes.

"I've never heard of it."

Then—voices.
Distant. Many.
Human.

You freeze.

He turns toward the sound.

"Come with me."

A pause.

"Trust me."

"Whatever you think is out there… you're past it now."

He walks forward.

A town appears beyond the trees.

4.title: End of the Forest
4.text:
Hours pass.
Pain fades.
Trees thin.

Then—something impossible.

A path.
Made by humans.

You were lied to.

choices.result: You step onto the path. It feels too clean compared to everything you've known.
You slow down and study the surroundings. Someone has been maintaining this area.
You inspect the path carefully. This wasn't made by accident. It was maintained.

5.title: A World Beyond
5.text:
Trees end.

Then—buildings.
Not ruins.
Real ones.

People.
Noise.
Life.

Greyhaven.

The world is still alive.

choices.result: You take everything in. This place runs on rules you don't yet understand.
You focus on the people. They don't look like survivors. They look... normal.
You force yourself to act steady. No one here needs to see hesitation.

route_choice.title: Two Roads
route_choice.text:
Night in Greyhaven.

The Head watches the town.
Then you.

"You can stay."

"Or leave."

Simple choice.
Heavy consequences.

choices.result: You decide to stay. Not because it's safe, but because it's the first place that feels real.
You choose to leave. The unknown outside still calls louder than the town.

settler_recovery_event.title: A Place To Begin
settler_recovery_event.text:
Days pass.

The wounds from the forest slowly heal.

Greyhaven moves around you.

People work.
Children play.
Merchants argue.
Life continues.

No one treats you like a hero.
No one treats you like a threat.

Just another stranger trying to find a place.

The man who found you visits occasionally.

He answers some questions.
Avoids others.

Each answer only proves one thing.

The world is far larger than you were taught.

Eventually, the town grants you a small house near the edge of Greyhaven.

A bed.
A table.
A stove.

Nothing impressive.

But it is yours.

For the first time since leaving home, you sleep without fear.

A week later, the town healer finally clears you for work.

You spend the morning searching for opportunities.

Near the center of town stands a large wooden board covered in notices.

Job postings.
Requests.
Applications.

A new life has to start somewhere.

explorer_departure_event.title: Beyond Greyhaven
explorer_departure_event.text:
The decision surprises no one.

Not because people expected it.
Because few people care enough to stop you.

Greyhaven continues as it always has.

Merchants open their stalls.
Workers begin their routines.
Children race through streets that once seemed impossible to you.

Life moves forward.

A week ago, this town felt larger than the world.

Now it feels small.

Before leaving, you visit the Head one final time.

He studies you for a moment.

"Most people spend their lives searching for a place to stay."

His gaze drifts toward the eastern road.

"A few spend their lives searching for what lies beyond it."

Silence follows.

Then he hands you a folded map.

Old.
Worn.
Used.

The parchment stretches far beyond Greyhaven.

Roads.
Settlements.
Rivers.
Mountain ranges.

Places you've never heard of.

The kingdom is larger than anything you imagined.

Greyhaven sits near the far western edge.

A single dot among countless others.

"The world didn't end with your village."

"And it doesn't end here either."

The next morning, you leave.

No ceremony.
No farewell.

Just a road leading east.

For the first time in your life, no one knows where you'll be tomorrow.

Not even you.

6_settler.title: The Job Board
6_settler.text:
The board is larger than expected.

Dozens of notices cover the wood.

Some offer temporary work.
Others seek long-term employees.

For a moment, the number of choices feels overwhelming.

Back home, work was simple.
You hunted.
You gathered.
You survived.

Greyhaven is different.

People specialize.

They build entire lives around a single profession.

After reading through the postings, three opportunities stand out.

A librarian is seeking an assistant.

The town administration is looking for a clerk.

The treasury seeks an apprentice.

One choice could shape your new life.

choices.result: You take the library job. Quiet work, steady learning.
You join administration. Decisions flow through people like this.
You join the treasury. Trust is the real currency here.

6_explorer.title: The Open Road
6_explorer.text:
Days pass.

Greyhaven slowly disappears behind you.

The road stretches east.

Farmland replaces wilderness.

Small hamlets appear occasionally.

Travelers pass in both directions.

Most barely notice you.

A few offer greetings.

For the first time in your life, the world feels larger every day instead of smaller.

One afternoon, you reach a crossroads.

A weathered sign points toward several distant settlements.

You unfold the map the Head gave you.

The amount of land ahead is overwhelming.

You could spend years exploring it all.

For a moment, you stop walking.

Not because you're tired.

Because the realization finally settles in.

You are free to choose where this journey goes.

choices.text: Study the map and mark places worth investigating
choices.result: You begin studying routes, settlements, and landmarks. Every road tells a story.

choices.text: Talk with travelers about distant regions
choices.result: Most travelers enjoy sharing stories. Some sound impossible. Not all of them are.

choices.text: Take the longest route simply to see more of the world
choices.result: The efficient path isn't always the interesting one. You choose experience over speed.

job_selection_event.title: A New Routine
job_selection_event.text:
The application process is simple.

Within days, your new routine begins.

Greyhaven starts to feel familiar.

For the first time since leaving home, life stabilizes.

You are no longer surviving.
You are becoming part of something.

7_settler.title: First Days of Work
7_settler.text:
Your routine begins immediately.

Greyhaven doesn't wait.

You learn by doing.

People start recognizing you.
Not your story.
Just your presence.

You are inside the system now.

choices.result: You study records deeply. Patterns start forming faster.
You coordinate tasks. People begin listening naturally.
You track resources carefully. Mistakes drop quickly.

7_explorer.title: Roadside Rumors
7_explorer.text:
The farther east you travel, the busier the roads become.

Merchants.
Travelers.
Messengers.

Everyone seems to be heading somewhere.

At a roadside shelter, conversation drifts toward the same topic.

A merchant caravan is expected to pass through soon.

Some speak of valuable cargo.

Others speak of strange artifacts.

A few claim the merchant has spent years collecting unusual objects from across the kingdom.

Most of the stories sound exaggerated.

Then again, most stories do.

The next morning, you continue down the road.

Hours later, dark clouds gather overhead.

In the distance, you spot wagon tracks heading toward a narrow mountain pass.

The same route the merchant caravan was supposed to take.

choices.text: Study the rumors and compare different stories
choices.result: Most stories contradict each other. The interesting part is where they don't.

choices.text: Insert yourself into conversations and become the center of them
choices.result: People reveal more information when they want your attention.

choices.text: Keep moving despite the worsening conditions
choices.result: The road grows rough, but you endure it. Distance matters less than persistence.

settler_conflict_event.title: The Fence Line Dispute
settler_conflict_event.text:
Two neighbors argue near the edge of town.

A fence was repainted for the festival.

One claims the other crossed the property line.

A small issue.
But it’s already spreading attention.

People gather.
Watching.
Waiting.

Then they notice you.

The argument slows.
Not solved.
Just observed.

wagon_incident_event.title: The Broken Wagon
wagon_incident_event.text:
The storm arrives faster than expected.

Rain turns the road into mud.

Travelers begin seeking shelter wherever they can.

You continue forward.

Then you hear shouting.

A wagon has overturned near a narrow section of road.

Crates lie scattered across the mud.

One horse struggles against tangled harnesses.

Several travelers are already attempting to help.

At the center of the chaos stands an older merchant.

Drenched.
Covered in mud.
Still somehow giving orders.

The next hour becomes a blur.

Ropes are pulled.

Supplies are recovered.

The horse is freed.

The wagon itself survives, though barely.

By nightfall a small camp forms beside the road.

The merchant eventually approaches your fire carrying a wooden box.

Most people would've kept walking.

He sits across from you.

So I'd like to offer something in return.

He opens the box.

Inside rest three objects.

An old leather journal.

A silver signet ring.

And a wooden chicken.

You stare at the chicken.

The merchant notices.

Everyone does that.

He points to the journal.

Bought that from a ruin explorer years ago.

He claimed it was more valuable than gold.

Most of the pages are blank.

A strange symbol is burned into the cover.

The merchant then lifts the ring.

This belonged to someone important once.

No idea who.

But people tend to treat the wearer differently.

Finally he picks up the wooden chicken.

It rattles when shaken.

One leg is shorter than the other.

Don't underestimate the chicken.

Why?

The merchant pauses.

I honestly don't know.

Then why keep it?

Because every owner I've met refused to throw it away.

That answer somehow raises more questions than it solves.

The merchant places all three objects back into the box.

Take one.

Every traveler carries something.

The question is what kind of traveler you become.

8_settler.title: Festival Night
8_settler.text:
Greyhaven spends the day in preparation.

Streets are cleaned.
Lanterns are hung.
Stalls are built in silence that slowly turns into anticipation.

By evening, the town transforms.

The festival begins almost without warning.

Light floods the streets.
Music replaces conversation.
The same place you’ve been walking through suddenly feels unfamiliar.

Alive in a different way.

People move in celebration, but small issues still appear beneath the surface.
Nothing serious.
Just enough to notice if you're paying attention.

choices.text: Notice issues early and quietly fix them
choices.result: You pick up on small problems before they grow. The festival runs smoother without anyone knowing why.

choices.text: Step in and direct people to keep things running
choices.result: You start coordinating people without formal authority. Things fall into order under your direction.

choices.text: Join the crowd and help wherever needed
choices.result: You blend into the festival, helping where needed. For once, you're not watching from the outside.

8_explorer.title: The Merchant's Offer
8_explorer.text:
The storm has weakened.

Only the sound of rain remains.

The merchant leaves the box between you.

Three objects.

Three stories.

Perhaps three futures.

The journal waits silently.

The ring catches the firelight.

The chicken looks as ridiculous as before.

The merchant folds his arms.

Only one.

Choose carefully.

Or don't.

He seems equally entertained by either option.

choices.result: The cover feels older than it should. As your fingers touch the symbol, curiosity replaces hesitation.
The ring is surprisingly heavy. Whatever name it once carried has long been forgotten.

The merchant laughs.

I knew someone would eventually choose the chicken.

The chicken rattles mysteriously.

8_settler_event.title: Afterglow
8_settler_event.text:
The night settles slowly.

Lanterns dim.
Music fades into distance.
Streets grow quiet again.

But something lingers.

The festival didn't just happen around people.
It happened because of them.

And somehow, you were part of that system now.
Not outside it.
Inside it.

9_settler.title: The First Real Problem
9_settler.text:
Greyhaven's system doesn't stay calm for long.

A problem surfaces in your workplace.

Something small at first.
A missing record.
A misfiled document.
A delayed approval.

But it starts affecting other departments.

People begin asking questions.

And your name is attached to part of the chain.

choices.text: Fix it directly through your job role
choices.result: You dig into the records and correct the issue at its source. The system stabilizes quietly.

choices.text: Coordinate people to resolve it quickly
choices.result: You organize people across departments. The issue gets resolved faster, but it exposes weak points.

choices.text: Take responsibility and reassure everyone involved
choices.result: You step in calmly and take responsibility where needed. Trust in your role increases.

9_explorer.title: An Unusual Companion
9_explorer.text:
Morning arrives.

The storm has passed.

The merchant continues along his route.

You continue along yours.

The road stretches east once again.

Yet something feels different.

Not because the world changed.

Because you now carry something that wasn't yours before.

Several days pass.

Fields become hills.

Roads become trails.

Travel becomes quieter.

During a stop beneath an old stone marker, your attention drifts toward the item you chose.

For a moment, you wonder if the merchant knew more than he admitted.

choices.result: You spend hours studying the object. Every detail only creates more questions.

artifact_result.journal: The journal feels older than it should. While turning its pages, you discover a blank page that definitely wasn't there before.
artifact_result.ring: A tiny inscription is carved into the inner band of the ring. The language is unfamiliar, but the craftsmanship is unmistakably deliberate.
artifact_result.chicken: The wooden chicken rattles when shaken. You inspect it thoroughly. No compartment. No mechanism. Yet the sound remains.

choices.text: Ask travelers if they recognize it
choices.result: Most travelers shrug and move on. A few hesitate before answering.

artifact_result.journal: An elderly traveler sees the symbol on the cover and immediately loses interest in the conversation. He refuses to explain why.
artifact_result.ring: One merchant recognizes the ring instantly. Before you can ask anything, he apologizes and leaves without another word.
artifact_result.chicken: Several travelers laugh at the chicken. One of them stops laughing after looking at it closely and quietly advises you not to lose it.

choices.text: Keep moving and see where it leads
choices.result: Answers can wait. Roads have a habit of revealing things in their own time.

artifact_result.journal: That night, you wake to find the journal lying open beside your bedroll despite being packed away before sleep.
artifact_result.ring: Throughout the journey, strangers seem more willing to approach you than before. None explain why.
artifact_result.chicken: Near sunset, the chicken falls from your pack. You could swear it was secured moments earlier.

9_settler_event.title: After the Correction
9_settler_event.text:
The problem doesn't spread.

But it doesn't disappear either.

It gets recorded.
Tracked.
Analyzed.

Greyhaven doesn't forget who touched what.

Your involvement is now part of the system's memory.

Whether it was good or bad depends on what comes next.

10_settler.title: Evaluation Notice
10_settler.text:
You are called in unexpectedly.

Not for punishment.
Not for praise.

For review.

Greyhaven's system has been tracking your performance.
Your consistency.
Your decisions.
Your alignment with your assigned role.

Three departments are present in the evaluation.
Each one represents a different path you could have taken.

They don’t ask if you succeeded.

They ask how you function inside the system.

choices.text: Emphasize your technical accuracy and problem-solving
choices.result: Your precision is noted. Your ability to stabilize information systems is recognized.

choices.text: Highlight your coordination and decision-making impact
choices.result: Your ability to manage people and flow of decisions becomes your defining trait.

choices.text: Focus on reliability and trust within the system
choices.result: Your consistency and reliability become your strongest asset in their evaluation.

10_explorer.title: The Silent Waystation
10_explorer.text:
The road stretches onward for days.

The hills grow steeper.
The settlements become rarer.

Travelers pass less frequently now.

The kingdom feels larger with every step.

Then, near sunset, you spot stone rising from the landscape.

At first you mistake it for a ruined wall.

Then another appears.

And another.

A structure.

Old.
Abandoned.

Built beside a crossroads that no longer appears on any map you've seen.

The place should feel forgotten.

Instead, it feels patient.

The main building has partially collapsed.

Weather has worn away carvings.

Plants have reclaimed sections of the stone.

Yet signs of travelers remain.

Old campfires.
Footprints.
Supplies left behind.

People still come here.

Just not many.

Near the entrance stands a weathered plaque.

Most of the inscription has been destroyed.

Only a single line remains readable.

For those who seek beyond the known roads.

You read the words twice.

Something about them feels intentional.

As though the message was waiting for someone.

Waiting for anyone willing to keep walking.

Your gaze drifts toward the artifact you've carried since meeting the merchant.

For a brief moment, it almost feels connected.

Not to this place.

To whatever lies beyond it.

The evening grows darker.

You decide to investigate before continuing your journey.

choices.result: You spend hours examining the remaining carvings. Most are damaged beyond recognition, but fragments hint at routes and places no modern traveler seems to remember.

choices.text: Speak with the travelers resting nearby
choices.result: You spend the evening listening rather than searching. Every traveler tells a different story about the waystation, but all of them returned for a reason.

choices.text: Determine who benefits from this crossroads
choices.result: Roads shape kingdoms. Trade shapes roads. The more you observe, the clearer it becomes that influence travels farther than armies.
You navigate unstable passages most travelers avoid. Hidden paths reveal parts of the structure untouched for years.

choices.text: Endure the hazards and search every corner
choices.result: The search is exhausting. Dust, unstable floors, and endless dead ends test your endurance. You keep going long after most people would have stopped.

first_promotion_event.title: Promotion
first_promotion_event.text:
The review concludes.

The department recognizes your contributions.

Not because of a single achievement.

Because of consistency.

Months of reliable work have begun producing results.

Your supervisor presents the decision.

Additional responsibilities.

Greater authority.

A higher position within Greyhaven's system.

The promotion is not celebrated with speeches.

No crowd gathers.

No ceremony takes place.

Work simply continues.

Yet something has changed.

People now expect more from you.

And for the first time since arriving in Greyhaven, you are no longer considered new.

You have become part of the town's foundation.

second_promotion_event.title: Senior Appointment
second_promotion_event.text:
The decision takes longer this time.

The review involves multiple departments.

Reports.

Evaluations.

Recommendations.

Your name appears repeatedly.

Eventually the conclusion is reached.

You are offered one of the highest positions available within your profession.

The responsibility is significant.

Mistakes made at this level affect more than a single workplace.

They affect Greyhaven itself.

The appointment comes with authority.

Influence.

Expectations.

You think back to the stranger who first entered this town.

The person who knew nothing about the world beyond a forgotten village.

That person feels distant now.

Greyhaven is no longer a place you live in.

It is a place you help shape.

Whether that future succeeds or fails will depend partly on you.

class_event.title: The Old Cartographer
class_event.text:
The waystation isn't entirely abandoned.

As night falls, you notice an old man sitting beneath a damaged archway.

Maps cover the stone beside him.

Hundreds of markings.
Routes.
Notes.
Places.

He doesn't look surprised to see you.

Another traveler.

His eyes drift toward the artifact you're carrying.

Interesting.

You ask if he lives here.

He laughs.

No one lives here.

People pass through.

That's different.

Silence settles.

Eventually he rolls up one of his maps.

Most travelers think roads are what separate people.

They're wrong.

He points toward the crossroads outside.

Roads reveal people.

Follow enough of them and patterns appear.

His finger moves across several notes written on the map.

Some become Chroniclers.

People obsessed with recording what others overlook.

Another mark.

Some become Emissaries.

They connect strangers faster than roads ever could.

Another.

Some become Kingmakers.

Not rulers.

The people who decide who rules.

Another.

Some become Pilgrims.

The world rarely rewards them.

Yet somehow they outlast everyone else.

His finger stops at the final symbol.

And some become Pathfinders.

The people who walk where roads don't exist yet.

The old man studies you.

Not your face.

Your gear.
Your posture.
Your decisions.

Then he smiles.

The funny thing is...

Most travelers become one long before they realize it.

The fire crackles.

The crossroads stand silent beyond the darkness.

For the first time, you wonder what kind of traveler you've already become.

11_settler.title: Familiar Faces
11_settler.text:
Months have passed.

Greyhaven no longer feels unfamiliar.

People greet you by name.

Shopkeepers remember your orders.

Children wave when you pass.

The town has quietly accepted you.

Then a concern reaches the council.

Several families have recently arrived from smaller settlements.

Housing is becoming limited.

Resources remain stable.

Space does not.

The council begins discussing possible solutions.

Arguments form quickly.

Some want expansion.

Others want caution.

For the first time, people ask what you think.

choices.result: Greyhaven grows because people choose to come here. We should prepare for them.
Growth without planning creates problems later.
The people already here should not be ignored.

11_explorer.title: The Repeating Symbol
11_explorer.text:
Months of exploration have taken you across forgotten roads, ruined watchtowers, and abandoned landmarks.

Most discoveries fade into memory.

One does not.

A symbol.

At first it seemed meaningless.

A carving in a ruined wall.

A mark on an ancient map.

A faded design hidden within a damaged monument.

Now it has appeared again.

And again.

Different locations.

Different histories.

Different ages.

Yet somehow the same symbol remains.

It should not exist in places this far apart.

The coincidence is becoming difficult to ignore.

choices.result: You begin recording every appearance of the symbol.
You gather stories and legends connected to the symbol.

choices.text: Investigate who may be using it
choices.result: You search for organizations or individuals connected to the mark.
You continue the search despite the difficult journey.
You create a network of clues linking the symbol together.

11_settler_event.title: The Head's Position
11_settler_event.text:
The Head attends the meeting.

He listens more than he speaks.

Eventually he makes a decision.

Expansion will begin slowly.

Controlled.

Measured.

Nobody leaves completely satisfied.

Yet nobody leaves angry either.

The Head notices you watching.

For a moment, it almost seems like he's evaluating you.

11_explorer_event.title: A Pattern
11_explorer_event.text:
The more evidence you gather, the stranger the pattern becomes.

The symbol appears across regions that should have no connection to one another.

Different cultures.

Different histories.

Different centuries.

Yet the same mark survives.

Not frequently.

Just enough to leave a trail.

Someone wanted it to be found.

Or remembered.

12_settler.title: Increased Responsibility
12_settler.text:
The expansion project affects every department.

Additional responsibilities appear.

Mistakes now carry larger consequences.

Your supervisor assigns you a task normally given to senior workers.

The assignment will directly affect Greyhaven's future plans.

choices.result: You personally verify every record and detail. Accuracy matters more than speed.
You coordinate communication across departments, keeping the project moving efficiently.
You ensure every resource allocation is accounted for and properly documented.

12_explorer.title: The Silent Settlement
12_explorer.text:
Following the trail leads you to an abandoned settlement.

The buildings remain standing.

The roads remain visible.

Nothing suggests violence.

Nothing suggests disaster.

The people simply left.

Years ago.

Perhaps decades.

Among the abandoned structures, you discover the symbol once again.

Carved into stone near the settlement's center.

choices.result: You search for written explanations behind the settlement's disappearance.
You search for letters and traces left behind by former residents.
You investigate the people who guided the settlement.
You expand the search into the surrounding wilderness.
You follow the routes used during the settlement's final days.

12_settler_event.title: Notice
12_settler_event.text:
Your work succeeds.

Quietly.

Efficiently.

The kind of success that becomes invisible because nothing goes wrong.

The Head receives the final reports.

Your name appears among them.

Again.

12_explorer_event.title: Gone Searching
12_explorer_event.text:
Fragments of evidence begin forming a picture.

The settlement did not collapse.

It departed.

Groups left in stages.

Each one following the same direction.

Each one searching for something.

No record explains what they hoped to find.

Only that they believed it was important enough to leave everything behind.

13_settler.title: Voices of Greyhaven
13_settler.text:
Greyhaven continues growing.

New workers arrive.

New merchants arrive.

New opinions arrive.

The town begins changing.

Some citizens welcome it.

Others fear losing what Greyhaven once was.

Arguments spread through taverns.

Markets.

Meetings.

No crisis exists.

Yet tension does.

choices.result: You listen carefully to both sides before forming conclusions.
You encourage discussion before disagreements become divisions.
You focus on maintaining stability while emotions run high.

13_explorer.title: The Missing Expedition
13_explorer.text:
Old archives reference an expedition that vanished years ago.

Experienced explorers.

Well supplied.

Well prepared.

They departed with a clear objective.

None returned.

Recently, however, travelers have reported signs that the expedition may have reached its destination.

The reports are impossible to verify.

Yet one detail appears repeatedly.

The symbol.

choices.result: You gather everything known about the missing explorers.
You collect stories from travelers who claim to have seen evidence.
You search for the people who supported the expedition.
You travel along the same dangerous route they once followed.
You reconstruct the expedition's likely objective.

13_settler_event.title: Rumors
13_settler_event.text:
Rumors begin spreading.

Nothing dangerous.

Nothing proven.

People claim the Head is planning something larger.

Expansion.

Political recognition.

Greater authority.

No one knows the truth.

Yet people continue talking.

13_explorer_event.title: The Same Trail
13_explorer_event.text:
The evidence leaves little room for doubt.

The expedition was investigating the symbol.

Just like you.

Their notes become increasingly focused as they approach their final destination.

Then the records stop.

No explanation.

No conclusion.

Only silence.

14_settler.title: The King's Messenger
14_settler.text:
A royal messenger arrives.

The kingdom has finally noticed Greyhaven.

Reports of growth have reached the capital.

Recognition may soon follow.

The town celebrates.

Most people.

Not everyone.

The messenger requests private meetings with local leaders.

Including the Head.

Including you.

Questions are asked.

About growth.

About leadership.

About Greyhaven's future.

Something feels unusual.

Not wrong.

Hidden.

choices.result: You answer openly, believing honesty is the strongest foundation.
You choose your words carefully, aware that details can change outcomes.
You speak with confidence, presenting Greyhaven as a success story.

14_explorer.title: The Watchers
14_explorer.text:
As your investigation continues, a strange pattern emerges.

People begin appearing wherever you travel.

Different faces.

Different names.

Yet the questions remain similar.

What have you found?

Where are you going?

What do you know about the symbol?

They never threaten you.

They never reveal who they work for.

But someone is paying attention.

choices.result: You focus on gathering information about the mysterious observers.
You attempt to earn their confidence.
You search for the purpose behind their actions.
You refuse to be distracted from the investigation.
You move carefully to prevent them from tracking your progress.

judgment_event.title: Judgment
judgment_event.text:
The misunderstanding begins with paperwork.

Then conversations.

Then assumptions.

Reports sent to the capital contain contradictions.

Statements become distorted.

Words lose context.

The messenger believes the Head is consolidating power.

Some citizens believe the Head intends to replace local council authority.

Others believe the kingdom intends to seize control of Greyhaven.

Nobody possesses the full truth.

Yet everyone believes they do.

The town divides.

Not into enemies.

Into interpretations.

Meetings become arguments.

Arguments become accusations.

For the first time since arriving in Greyhaven, uncertainty spreads through every street.

Then the messenger delivers a formal notice.

A final review will determine Greyhaven's future status.

The Head becomes the center of the controversy.

And somehow...

So do you.

14_explorer_event.title: Questions Without Answers
14_explorer_event.text:
You never learn everything.

But one fact becomes clear.

The watchers are searching for the same truth.

They know about the symbol.

They know about the missing expedition.

And they know far more than they admit.

You are not the only person following this trail.

15_settler.title: Final Responsibility
15_settler.text:
The review approaches.

The town remains tense.

Work continues anyway.

It always does.

Your department receives one final critical assignment.

Failure could damage Greyhaven's position.

Success could strengthen it.

Everyone is watching.

choices.result: Facts should decide this.
People need direction before they need answers.
A town survives because people trust each other.

15_explorer.title: The First Fragment
15_explorer.text:
The trail finally leads somewhere tangible.

Hidden beneath ruins older than any map, you discover a fragment.

Stone.

Covered in symbols.

Including the one you have been following.

For the first time, all the clues connect.

The abandoned settlement.

The missing expedition.

The watchers.

None of them were separate mysteries.

They were pieces of the same puzzle.

choices.result: You focus on understanding the fragment's meaning.
You believe important discoveries should not remain hidden.
You focus on what this discovery could mean for the world.
You prioritize keeping the fragment safe.
You immediately begin searching for more fragments.

head_conflict.title: The Head's Decision
head_conflict.text:
The final review takes place in the council hall.

Citizens gather.

Department leaders gather.

The royal messenger arrives carrying the kingdom's judgment.

The Head stands before them all.

Calm.

Unmoved.

The accusations are repeated.

Some claim Greyhaven has grown beyond its place.

Some claim the Head has gathered too much authority.

Others claim the kingdom simply fears an independent town.

For hours, arguments fill the hall.

Then the messenger presents a solution.

Greyhaven may receive official recognition.

But only if significant authority is transferred away from the Head and placed under direct royal oversight.

The room falls silent.

The decision would protect Greyhaven's future.

But it would also change the town forever.

For the first time, the Head turns toward you.

'You've lived among these people.'

'You've seen this town grow.'

'Tell them what should happen.'

choices.result: Systems should survive individuals. Greyhaven's future must be protected, even if change is required.
Leadership built this town. Trust should not be discarded because of rumors and fear.
Greyhaven was built through cooperation. The answer should unite people, not divide them.

15_explorer_event.title: A Name
15_explorer_event.text:
The fragment is incomplete.

Most of its message has been lost.

Only fragments remain.

Yet one phrase survives.

A name.

The Final Horizon.

The missing expedition searched for it.

The abandoned settlement pursued it.

The watchers seek it.

And now...

So do you.

settler_scholar_ending.title: The Scholar of Greyhaven
settler_scholar_ending.text:
The review eventually concludes.

The misunderstanding fades.

The rumors disappear.

The truth proves less dramatic than the stories people told.

Greyhaven receives recognition from the kingdom.

Life moves forward.

As years pass, your reputation grows.

Not as a leader.

Not as a politician.

As a source of knowledge.

Records improve under your guidance.

Systems become more efficient.

Future generations learn from information you helped preserve.

People seek your advice before making important decisions.

Not because you hold authority.

Because your understanding has earned respect.

When newcomers arrive in Greyhaven, they find a town built upon lessons learned rather than mistakes repeated.

And hidden within countless records and decisions lies your influence.

Quiet.

Invisible.

Enduring.

You left home searching for a future.

You found it in understanding.

Ending Unlocked:
The Scholar of Greyhaven

settler_leader_ending.title: The Voice of Greyhaven
settler_leader_ending.text:
The crisis eventually passes.

Recognition is granted.

The town survives.

But people remember who stood forward when uncertainty spread.

Your name appears often in those memories.

Years pass.

Greyhaven continues growing.

New districts appear.

New opportunities emerge.

New challenges follow.

Again and again, people look toward you.

Not because they must.

Because they choose to.

You become one of the town's most respected leaders.

Someone capable of bringing people together when opinions divide them.

Someone willing to make difficult decisions when others hesitate.

The Head eventually steps aside.

Time reaches everyone.

But Greyhaven never lacks direction.

A new generation carries the town forward.

And your influence remains part of its foundation.

You left home searching for a place to belong.

You found it among people.

Ending Unlocked:
The Voice of Greyhaven

settler_community_ending.title: The Heart of Greyhaven
settler_community_ending.text:
The review concludes.

The arguments fade.

The town heals.

Not because everyone agrees.

Because people choose to move forward together.

Years pass.

Greyhaven grows steadily.

Children become adults.

Families expand.

New faces arrive.

Old faces disappear.

Through it all, one thing remains constant.

You.

You never seek authority.

You never chase recognition.

Yet nearly everyone knows your name.

The shopkeepers.

The workers.

The families.

The people who quietly keep Greyhaven alive.

When disputes arise, people trust you.

When celebrations begin, people invite you.

When difficult times arrive, people rely on you.

Not because of your position.

Because of your character.

Greyhaven becomes more than a town.

It becomes a community.

And in countless ways, you helped make that possible.

You left home searching for somewhere to live.

You found a place to call home.

Ending Unlocked:
The Heart of Greyhaven
